LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SEYMOUR HOLTZMAN, HOLTZMAN FAMILY PARTNERSHIP'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS SEYMOUR HOLTZMAN AND HOLTZMAN FAMILY PARTNERSHIP'S MOTION TO QUASH BASED ON LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION IS DENIED AND THEIR MOTION TO STAY THIS ACTION BASED ON INCONVENIENT FORUM IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Castricone, Esq. 415-875-3183 of Gordon & Rees for Plaintiff.
Brian Hafter, Esq. 415-964-5207 of Rimon P.C. for Defendant SEYMOUR HOLTZMAN, HOLTZMAN FAMILY PARTNERSHIP.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs parties the issue with efiling and the entry of default. Plaintiff argues, reports chronology of events and submits copies of documents for judicial notice. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SEYMOUR HOLTZMAN, HOLTZMAN FAMILY PARTNERSHIP's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Seymour Holtzman and Holtzman Family Partnership's motion to quash based on lack of personal jurisdiction is denied and their motion to stay this action based on inconvenient forum is granted. This case is stayed pending the completion of proceedings in Florida on the claims alleged by plaintiffs Joseph Mizrachi and JAL Group, L.P. Plaintiffs have demonstrated that defendants have sufficient minimum contacts with California to warrant specific jurisdiction over defendants in this case. Defendants purposefully availed themselves of the benefits of California through their extensive dealings with plaintiffs in acquiring interests in the Brentwood entities which involved retaining California attorney James Smith and Kilpatrick Townsend & Stockton LLP. Notwithstanding that this court may exercise personal jurisdiction over defendants, plaintiffs' claims should be litigated in Florida, not in California. Florida is a suitable alternative forum and the parties are currently engaged in litigation in that state. The public and private factors strongly favor litigation of plaintiffs' claims in Florida where the individual parties reside. California has minimal interest in this case which does not involve any California residents and is based on an alleged oral contract that, although it concerns payment of fees to California residents, was allegedly entered into and allegedly breached in Florida. There is no reason to believe that any party would be disadvantaged by litigating plaintiffs' claims in Florida and plaintiffs do not so argue. Apparently the parties are not aware that the default of defendants was entered on December 12, 2018, prior to the defendants' filing of this motion. Based on the papers filed on this motion, the court on its own motion and in the interests of justice and consistent with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits, vacates the defaults entered against defendants.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
